title: Toona ciliata
taxonomy: Toona ciliata is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae. It is commonly known as the Australian cedar or southern walnut. The species belongs to the genus Toona, which consists of four to six species of trees. The genus Toona is part of the order Sapindales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Toona ciliata is native to southeastern Australia and Tasmania.
uses: Toona ciliata, also known as the Eastern Red Cedar or Australian Red Cedar, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's bark and leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, Toona ciliata is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and nausea. The plant's essential oils have also been used to treat skin conditions, including acne and wounds. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties have been used to treat infections and promote wound healing.

intro: Toona ciliata, also known as the Southeast Asian mahogany or saigon mahogany, is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia and India, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The bark and leaves of Toona ciliata have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been found to exhibit cardiotonic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. Toona ciliata is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder, and is used in traditional herbal remedies to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health.
